      "title": "Weak origin validation allows substring matching attack",
      "description": "Line 15 uses `discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin) === -1` to validate message origin. This allows an attacker to bypass validation.       "suggestion": "Use proper URL parsing and strict equality check: `new URL(discourseUrl).origin === e.origin` or `discourseUrl === e.origin`"
    },

      "severity": "high",

      "title": "Raw HTML from RSS feed inserted without sanitization",
      "description": "Line 34 passes `content = CGI.unescapeHTML(i.content.scrub)` directly to `TopicEmbed.import()`. The CGI.unescapeHTML call actually expands HTML entities, and while scrub() removes invalid UTF-8, neither operation sanitizes malicious HTML/JavaScript. This allows XSS injection via feed content.",
      "suggestion": "Use Rails sanitization helper: `content = Rails::Html::FullSanitizer.new.sanitize(i.content)` or similar before passing to import"
    },

      "description": "Line 29 uses `open(SiteSetting.feed_polling_url)` without validating the URL. An attacker could trigger requests to internal services.",
      "suggestion": "Validate the URL scheme and host before fetching: whitelist https/http, validate against internal IP ranges, or use a safer HTTP client with built-in protections"
